mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0% signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending, which forces S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 9.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect immediate capacity utilization improvements and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of greater than 0.5% are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Ultra-low policy rates combined with stabilizing unemployment rates eliminate liquidity constraints and restore corporate cash flow expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative average 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% and UNRATE declines.

mechanism_narrative: Negative real GDP growth confirms aggregate demand contraction and earnings recession, which systematically drives S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices alongside stagnant policy rates trigger expectations of future monetary tightening and margin compression, forcing S&P 500 forward returns negative over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL with unchanged FEDFUNDS correlates with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in a majority of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly signal economic resilience and wage growth, which immediately lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of monthly observations.
